prerequisites: An AWS account with an Amazon DynamoDB table (or tables) you want to read from.
An AWS IAM identity (user or role) with read permissions on the target DynamoDB tables. AWS grants access by attaching identity-based IAM policies that allow DynamoDB actions on the resources.
The AWS Region where your DynamoDB tables live, since each region has its own DynamoDB endpoint (for example dynamodb.us-east-1.amazonaws.com).
An access key ID and secret access key for that IAM identity, used to sign DynamoDB API requests (unless you rely on an instance/role-provided credential chain).

authMethods.name: AWS IAM access key (access key ID + secret access key)
authMethods.summary: Authenticate by supplying an AWS access key ID and secret access key under the credentials field. These map to the connector secret fields credentials.access_key_id and credentials.secret_access_key. AWS uses them to cryptographically sign DynamoDB API requests with Signature Version 4. The IAM identity behind the keys must have an identity-based policy granting read access to the target DynamoDB tables.

setupSteps.title: Create or identify an IAM identity with DynamoDB read access
setupSteps.body: In the AWS IAM console, use an existing IAM user/role or create one, then attach an identity-based IAM policy that allows read access to the DynamoDB tables you want to sync. AWS controls access by attaching JSON policy documents to the identity that allow DynamoDB actions on the chosen resources.

setupSteps.title: Generate an access key ID and secret access key
setupSteps.body: For the IAM user, create an access key. Record the access key ID and the secret access key (the secret is shown only once). Supply these as the connector credentials.access_key_id and credentials.secret_access_key secret fields. If you leave the credentials field empty, the connector falls back to the default AWS credential resolution.

setupSteps.title: Set the AWS Region
setupSteps.body: Set the region config field to the AWS Region that hosts your DynamoDB tables (for example us-east-1). Specifying the region determines which DynamoDB endpoint is used, since each region maps to its own endpoint URL.

setupSteps.title: Optionally set a custom endpoint
setupSteps.body: Leave the endpoint config field empty to use the standard regional DynamoDB endpoint. Set it only when connecting to a non-default or local endpoint (for example DynamoDB Local at http://localhost:8000).

setupSteps.title: Optionally tune table discovery and attribute handling
setupSteps.body: Use ignore_missing_read_permissions_tables to skip tables the identity cannot read instead of failing, and use reserved_attribute_names to declare attribute names that need special handling. Both are optional config fields; set them only if your tables require it.

sources.title: Identity and Access Management for Amazon DynamoDB
AWS Regions and endpoints for DynamoDB
